shows.title: Paranormal Investigations / Training the Mind's Eye
shows.first_half: First Half: Guest host Ryan Wrecker (email) welcomes paranormal investigator Chip Coffey to discuss all things spiritual and supernatural. Coffey will explore the afterlife, ghosts, and how reincarnation remains a controversial topic. He'll also talk about the many investigations he has been on and where they took place.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Author Hakim Isler will share his experiences with remote viewing, mind-sight training, and holistic self-development. Isler works to bridge ancient disciplines with modern scientific inquiry, and to empower individuals through experiential learning.

shows.title: Crime Scene Cleaning / Animal Afterlife
shows.first_half: First Half: Biohazard expert Ethan Phearson will explain the process of cleaning up crime and trauma scenes. He'll detail what he deals with once authorities are done with the site. Phearson will also talk about suicides, homicides, and the many events that take place before and after he is called in to tidy up.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Rob Gutro is a paranormal investigator and pet medium who'll discuss how pets give you signs from the afterlife. He'll talk about the many aspects of animals in the afterlife, including if they understand their death and their communication skills.

shows.title: Natural Health Remedies / The Nature of Time
shows.first_half: First Half: Dr. Randi Shannon will explain the many ways we can use natural remedies to help with ailments and health issues in addition to using modern medicine. Dr. Shannon believes that supplementation and natural remedies are essential when it comes to everyday health.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Author and researcher Mike Ricksecker will discuss the reality of time, if it's indeed real. He'll show us the clues that have been left, from ancient symbolism and alchemy up through today's modern science and technology. Ricksecker will also talk about stacked time theory.

shows.title: Analyzing NDEs / Embracing Death
shows.first_half: First Half: German physicist, philosopher, and near-death experience researcher Dr. Vera Spillner will share how she became fascinated by NDEs after applying criminal interrogation techniques and statement analysis to testimonies. She'll also explore themes that connect to deep questions in physics and consciousness research.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Spiritual counselor and occultist Samantha Corfield, PhD, will discuss her work helping people overcome their fear of death. Dr. Corfield will explain such things as how to be a graceful ghost and what to do if you are facing a terminal diagnosis.

shows.title: Controlling Climate Narratives / Time-Travel Tourism
shows.first_half: First Half: Dr. Justin Mabie, an atmospheric physicist, will discuss his 17 years of experience working at the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), the US government's leading agency on climate change. He'll examine the trillion-dollar climate industry that's shaped and directed by NOAA science, and how those who control the science ultimately control the world.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Author James Wyllie will explore the idea of time travel reviving the world's most treasured ancient sites, including temples, the birthplace of Christ, and the vast expanse of the Roman Empire.

shows.title: Antarctic Mysteries / Open Lines
shows.first_half: Guest host Rich Berra (email) will speak with author Christopher Macklin about Antarctica, the mysteries that surround it, and its scientific perplexity. Macklin will talk about the many people that have visited this frigid destination and the possible reasons for them returning visibly shaken and deeply alarmed. Followed by Open Lines in the latter half—give Rich a call and discuss whatever's on your mind!
